title: Hierarchical Strategic Priors Via Cross-Fitted Encodings
summary: Driver and race IDs are high-cardinality and likely carry persistent strategic tendencies that simple frequency encoding misses.
rationale: Driver and race IDs are high-cardinality and likely carry persistent strategic tendencies that simple frequency encoding misses. Adjacent tabular literature and CatBoost docs both point to regularized/cross-fitted target statistics as a strong way to extract signal from such categories without catastrophic leakage.
implementation_hint: Build race-held-out, out-of-fold encodings with Bayesian shrinkage for Driver, Driver x Compound, Driver x Race, Race x Stint, Race x LapBin, and Year x Race. Feed them into the current tree stack and also train a raw-categorical CatBoost specialist, then average or rank-average with the best existing model.
expected_effect: Should complement handcrafted tyre-window features with stable strategic priors, especially for repeat drivers/tracks and unseen rare combinations that need shrinkage to the global mean.
risk: This is extremely leak-prone if folds are not grouped by race/year. Also unseen-category handling matters because test has fewer drivers than train; collapse rare levels and use only OOF encodings.
